zh.lifeSummary: 當Cynthia Ann Ryan於1827年出生在美國印第安納州克拉克時，她的父親Thomas Jefferson Ryan32歲，母親Cynthia Nolan32歲。她於1843年3月30日與Charles Cartwright在美國印第安納州克拉克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她於1850年住在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德劍橋大學默里 阿爾班尼。她去世於1859年9月28日在美國印第安納州哈里森享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Cynthia Ann Ryan于1827年出生在美国印第安纳州克拉克时，她的父亲Thomas Jefferson Ryan32岁，母亲Cynthia Nolan32岁。她于1843年3月30日与Charles Cartwright在美国印第安纳州克拉克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她于1850年住在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德劍橋大學默里 阿爾班尼。她去世于1859年9月28日在美国印第安纳州哈里森享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ryan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, John Patrick, Dermot, Liam, Paddy, Donovan, Eamon, Eamonn, Kieran, Parnell, Aileen, Brennan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Ryan , an Anglicized form of Ó Riaghain (modern Irish Ó Riain) ‘descendant of Riaghan’, a personal name probably based on the word rí ‘king’. Ryan is one of the commonest surnames in Ireland, though there has been considerable confusion with Regan .

variants.name: Ryon
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Irish   Ryan  .

variants.name: Ryans
meanings: Irish: variant of Ryan with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Mulryan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilriaghain ‘descendant of the devotee of (Saint) Riaghan’, a personal name probably based on the word rí ‘king’. The family were chieftains in Tipperary and Limerick. The name has sometimes been abbreviated to Ryan , which however can be the Anglicized form of two other Irish surnames.

variants.name: O'Ryan
meanings: (Leinster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Riagháin (modern Irish Ó Riain) ‘descendant of Riaghán’, a personal name probably based on the word rí ‘king’. See Ryan .
(Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilriaghain ‘descendant of Maoilriaghain’, a personal name meaning ‘follower of Riaghán’. See Ryan .

variants.name: Ryen
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of more than ten farmsteads, originally named with Old Norse rugr ‘rye’ + vin ‘meadow’.
Possibly also an altered form of Irish   Ryan  .

nameMeanings.name: Cynthia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek Kynthia, an epithet applied to the goddess Artemis, who was supposed to have been born on Mount Kynthos on the island of Delos. The mountain name is of pre-Greek origin. Cynthia was later used by the Roman poet Propertius as the name of the woman to whom he addressed his love poetry. The English given name was not used in the Middle Ages, but dates from the classical revival of the 17th and 18th centuries.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

historicEvents.title: The Massive Internal Improvements Act
historicEvents.description: The Massive Internal Improvements Act of 1836 loaned Indiana $10,000,000 to create infrastructure such as canals, railroads, and roads across the state. The act was signed by Whig Governor Noah Noble and passed by the Indiana General Assembly. However, the financial crisis known as the Panic of 1837 thwarted these plans as costs ballooned. Construction on the infrastructure was not completed and the state debt rapidly increased.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: Constitution of 1851
historicEvents.description: Due to the state’s financial crisis during the previous decade and growing criticism toward state government. Voters approve the Constitution of 1851 which forbade the state government from going into debt.

historicEvents.title: The Town of Santa Claus
historicEvents.description: The town of Santa Fe was denied their application for a United States Postal Service as a town of Santa Fe, Indiana, was already established. Several meetings were held and the name was changed to Santa Claus, Indiana. The United States Postal Service granted their application. Due to the name, the post office in Santa Claus continues to receive thousands of letters to Santa Claus from children around the world each December.

en.lifeSummary: When Cynthia Ann Ryan was born in 1827, in Clark, Indiana, United States, her father, Thomas Jefferson Ryan, was 32 and her mother, Cynthia Nolan, was 32. She married Charles Cartwright on 30 March 1843, in Clark, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She lived in New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, United States in 1850. She died on 28 September 1859, in Harrison, Indiana, United States, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cynthia Ann Ryan est née en 1827, à Clark, Indiana, États-Unis, son père, Thomas Jefferson Ryan, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Cynthia Nolan, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Charles Cartwright le 30 mars 1843, à Clark, Indiana, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle a vécu à New Albany, Floyd, Indiana, États-Unis en 1850. Elle est décédée le 28 septembre 1859, à Harrison, Indiana, États-Unis, à l’âge de 32 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Cynthia Ann Ryanが1827年にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークで生まれた時，父Thomas Jefferson Ryanは32歳，母Cynthia Nolanは32歳であった。1843年3月30日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークでCharles Cartwrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドニュー アルバニーに住んでいた。彼女は1859年9月28日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州ハーリソンで32歳で死去した。

ko.New Albany City, Floyd, Indiana, United: 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 뉴 알바니
ko.lifeSummary: Cynthia Ann Ryan(이)가 1827년 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Jefferson Ryan(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cynthia Nolan(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1843년 3월 30일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 Charles Cartwright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 뉴 알바니에서 살았다. 그녀는 1859년 9월 28일 미국 인디애나 주 해리슨에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cynthia Ann Ryan родилась в 1827 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Thomas Jefferson Ryan было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Cynthia Nolan, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Charles Cartwright 30 марта 1843 г. в местности кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она жила в местности ню албани, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла 28 сентября 1859 г. в местности харрисон, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 32 лет.
